1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→#
     4→# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
     5→# performance.
     6→#
     7→# Input:
     8→#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
     9→#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
    10→#       of libdsp.
    11→#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
    12→#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
    13→#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
    14→#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
    15→#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
    16→#       environment variable before using this script.
    17→#   $3: Simulator name
    18→#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
    19→#   $4: Simulation target
    20→#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
    21→#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
    22→#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
    23→#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
    24→#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
    25→#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
    26→#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
    27→#              will be verified in this mode.
    28→#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
    29→#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
    30→#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
    31→#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
    32→#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
    33→#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
    34→#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
    35→#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
    36→#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
    37→#       used to build the library.
    38→#   $7: Specified linker
    39→#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
    40→#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
    41→#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
    42→#
    43→# Example:
    44→#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
    45→#
    46→#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
    47→#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
    48→#
    49→#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
    50→#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
    51→#
    52→#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
    53→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
    54→#
    55→#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
    56→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    57→#
    58→#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
    59→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    60→#
    61→# Note:
    62→#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
    63→#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
    64→#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
    65→#   be used in the accuray test later.
    66→#
    67→################################################################################
    68→
    69→#######################################################
    70→# check the coverage of executed functions
    71→#######################################################
    72→function check_coverage {
    73→    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    74→    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    75→    cd ${GCOV_DIR}
    76→
    77→    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    78→    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
    79→        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
    80→        IFS=-
    81→        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
    82→        IFS=
    83→        coverage_status=""
    84→        is_coverage_fail=0
    85→
    86→        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
    87→        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
    88→            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
    89→                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
    90→                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
    91→                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
    92→                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
    93→                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
    94→                    # coverage_status+=""
    95→                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
    96→                        is_coverage_fail=1
    97→                    fi
    98→                fi
    99→
   100→                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")
   101→                break
   102→            fi
   103→        done
   104→
   105→        for called_fun in ${called_fun_list[@]}; do
   106→            fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
   107→            # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
   108→            if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
   109→                fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
   110→                coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
   111→                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
   112→                    is_coverage_fail=1
   113→                fi
   114→            fi
   115→        done
   116→
   117→        if [ ${is_coverage_fail} -eq 1 ]; then
   118→            coverage_fail_list+=("${coverage_status}")
   119→            echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COVERAGE_FAIL_LIST}
   120→            cp $(basename ${gcda/gcda/c.gcov}) ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
   121→        fi
   122→
   123→        # write coverage results into file
   124→        echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COV_CSV}
   125→    done
   126→
   127→    # go back to previous path
   128→    cd ${CURRENT_PATH}
   129→}
   130→
   131→#######################################################
   132→# collect the code size of each section
   133→#   $1: test function name
   134→#######################################################
   135→calculate_code_size () {
   136→    local log_file build_cmd tmp_size_log
   137→
   138→    echo -n "${1}," >> ${SIZE_CSV}
   139→    log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}_empty.log"
   140→    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG} -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1_empty.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${EMPTY_LIB} -lm"
   141→    #use the linker specified by the user
   142→    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
   143→        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
   144→    fi
   145→
   146→    # build the executable
   147→    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
   148→
   149→    # get the size info
   150→    tmp_size_log="${LOG_DIR}/size.log"
   151→    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx       | tail -1 > ${tmp_size_log}
   152→    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}_empty.adx | tail -1 >> ${tmp_size_log}
   153→
   154→    # calculate the size of each section
   155→    awk '
   156→        NR==1 {split($0,non_empty)}
   157→        NR==2 {split($0,empty)}
   158→        END {for(i=1;i<=5;i++) printf "%u,", non_empty[i] - empty[i]}
   159→    ' ${tmp_size_log} >> ${SIZE_CSV}
   160→    echo >> ${SIZE_CSV}  #new line
   161→}
   162→
   163→#######################################################
   164→# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
   165→#   $1: test function name
   166→#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
   167→#######################################################
   168→function run_and_check {
   169→    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
   170→    if [ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
   171→        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
   172→        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
   173→        return
   174→    fi
   175→
   176→    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VLN8 not enabled
   177→    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${1} =~ s4 ]]; then
   178→        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
   179→        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
   180→        return
   181→    fi
   182→
   183→    log "--- $1 ---"
   184→    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
   185→    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
   186→    #use the linker specified by the user
   187→    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
   188→        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
   189→    fi
   190→
   191→    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
   192→    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
   193→    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
   194→        # dump the disassembly
   195→        ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/t_${1}.objdump"
   196→
   197→        log "Start at $(date)"
   198→        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
   199→        ret=$?
   200→        log "End at $(date)"

250→   250→        fi
   251→   251→    else
   252→   252→        # build the executable fail
   253→   253→        accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
   254→   254→    fi
   255→   255→
   256→   256→    if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
   257→   257→        calculate_code_size "${1}"
   258→   258→    fi
   259→   259→}
   260→   260→
   261→   261→###############################################################################
   262→   262→# build and run the target configuration diagnosis program
   263→   263→#  $1: source file name of the program
   264→   264→###############################################################################
   265→   265→function target_cfg_diagnosis {
   266→   266→    local src_fname="target_cfg_diagnosis"
   267→   267→    log "--- ${src_fname} ---"
   268→   268→    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${src_fname}.log"
   269→   269→    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -o ${ADX_DIR}/$src_fname.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/$src_fname.c ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
   270→   270→    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
   271→   271→
   272→   272→    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
   273→   273→    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
   274→   274→        log "Start at $(date)"
   275→   275→        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${src_fname}.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
   276→   276→        ret=$?
   277→   277→        log "End at $(date)"
   278→   278→    fi
   279→   279→}
   280→   280→
   281→   281→#######################################################
   282→   282→# build the executables
   283→   283→#   $1: test function name
   284→   284→#######################################################
   285→   285→function build_executable {
   286→   286→    log "--- $1 ---"
   287→   287→    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"

1. toolchain /local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/

4. specify CPU AX45MPV and VLEN 1024 (don't touch other CPU)

   526→   526→    elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} != *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "ENA_LM" ]] ; then
   527→   527→        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V5)

   529→   529→    fi
   530→   530→fi
   531→   531→
   532→   532→# app and lib cflags for v-ext
   533→   533→if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
   534→   534→    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   535→   535→    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
   536→   536→        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FS_VS"
   537→   537→    fi
   538→   538→
   539→   539→    # As using v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default
   540→   540→    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FAST_ALGO"
   541→   541→
   542→   542→    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
   543→   543→        # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
   544→   544→        # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.

   546→   546→    fi

   548→   548→
   549→   549→# extra cflags for the environment with HVM
   550→   550→if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_HVM"* ]]; then

   552→   552→    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
   553→   553→fi
   554→   554→

   562→   562→    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -nostartfiles ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx25v/crt0.s"

   569→   569→    TOOLCHAIN_PATH=$(which ${CC})

   572→   572→        PREFIX=${CC%-*}

   574→   574→    else

   577→   577→    fi

   604→   604→    LINKED_LIB=""

   621→   621→E_TIME=0

445→#default CPU option
   446→if [[ "${CC}" == "nds32"* ]]; then

   465→    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]]; then

   470→        CPU_NAME="nx27v"

   473→    else

   487→    ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV=1

   501→if [ "${TEST_TYPE}" == "DAILY" ] && [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(SID|QEMU)$ ]] && [[ "${CC}" != *"clang" ]] && [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 0 ]]; then

   504→else

   506→fi

   508→DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -Wl,-gc-sections"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# parse_cfg.sh — INI-style config file parser for libnn target configurations
#
# Usage:
#   source config/parse_cfg.sh <config_file>
#   # or
#   eval "$(config/parse_cfg.sh <config_file>)"
#
# Reads an INI .cfg file and exports shell variables with the prefix CFG_.
# Section names map to variable prefixes:
#   [toolchain] → CFG_TOOLCHAIN_*
#   [qemu]      → CFG_QEMU_*
#   [board]     → CFG_BOARD_*
#
# Lines starting with # are comments. Empty lines are skipped.
# Values are trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace.
# Inline comments (# after value) are stripped.
################################################################################

__parse_cfg_file() {
    local cfg_file="${1}"

    if [[ -z "${cfg_file}" ]]; then
        echo "Error: No config file specified." >&2
        echo "Usage: source config/parse_cfg.sh <config_file>" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "${cfg_file}" ]]; then
        echo "Error: Config file '${cfg_file}' not found." >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local section=""
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "${line}" ]]; do
        # strip leading/trailing whitespace
        line="${line#"${line%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
        line="${line%"${line##*[![:space:]]}"}"

        # skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "${line}" ]] && continue
        [[ "${line}" == \#* ]] && continue

        # section header
        if [[ "${line}" =~ ^\[([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\]$ ]]; then
            section="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
            continue
        fi

        # key = value
        if [[ "${line}" =~ ^([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*(.*) ]]; then
            local key="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
            local val="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"

            # strip inline comments (# preceded by whitespace)
            val="${val%%[[:space:]]#*}"
            # strip trailing whitespace from value
            val="${val%"${val##*[![:space:]]}"}"

            # convert section and key to uppercase
            local section_upper
            section_upper=$(echo "${section}" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')
            local key_upper
            key_upper=$(echo "${key}" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')

            local varname="CFG_${section_upper}_${key_upper}"
            export "${varname}=${val}"
        fi
    done < "${cfg_file}"
}

# When sourced or executed with an argument, parse the config file
if [[ -n "${1}" ]]; then
    __parse_cfg_file "${1}"
fi

content.text: Now let me integrate `--config` into `build.sh`. I'll add the config parsing block right before the CPU case logic.

input.old_string:
#----- build switches -----
BUILD_LIBNN=1
BUILD_DOC=0

# common compilation flags

input.new_string:
#----- build switches -----
BUILD_LIBNN=1
BUILD_DOC=0
MAKE_INSTALL=1

# common compilation flags
CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#----- config file support -----
# Usage: ./build.sh --config <config_file> [extra_flags] [output_path]
# When --config is used, $1=--config, $2=config_file, $3=extra_flags, $4=output_path
if [[ "${1}" == "--config" ]]; then
    CONFIG_FILE="${2}"
    if [[ -z "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
        >&2 echo "Error: --config requires a config file path."
        exit 1
    fi

    # resolve config file path relative to this script's directory
    SCRIPT_DIR=$(readlink -f "$(dirname "${0}")")
    if [[ ! -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]] && [[ -f "${SCRIPT_DIR}/${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
        CONFIG_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/${CONFIG_FILE}"
    fi

    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/config/parse_cfg.sh" "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
        >&2 echo "Error: Failed to parse config file '${CONFIG_FILE}'."
        exit 1
    fi

    # map config values to build variables
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}"
    if [[ -n "${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_COMPILER}" ]]; then
        CC_FROM_CFG="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_COMPILER}"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_EXTRA_CFLAGS}" ]]; then
        CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_EXTRA_CFLAGS}"
    fi

    # determine CPU name from config file name (e.g., nx27v.cfg -> nx27v)
    CPU=$(basename "${CONFIG_FILE}" .cfg | tr A-Z a-z)

    # shift arguments: $3 becomes extra_flags, $4 becomes output_path
    EXTRA_FLAGS_ARG="${3}"
    OUTPUT_PATH_ARG="${4}"

    # apply the same CPU-specific flags as the normal path
    # (fall through to the CPU case logic below)
else
    # legacy positional argument mode
    EXTRA_FLAGS_ARG=""
    OUTPUT_PATH_ARG=""
fi

#convert CPU name to lower case
if [[ "${1}" != "--config" ]]; then
    CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)
fi

CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#----- config file support -----
# Usage: ./build.sh --config <config_file> [extra_flags] [output_path]
# When --config is used, $1=--config, $2=config_file, $3=extra_flags, $4=output_path
if [[ "${1}" == "--config" ]]; then
    CONFIG_FILE="${2}"
    if [[ -z "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
        >&2 echo "Error: --config requires a config file path."
        exit 1
    fi

    # resolve config file path relative to this script's directory
    SCRIPT_DIR=$(readlink -f "$(dirname "${0}")")
    if [[ ! -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]] && [[ -f "${SCRIPT_DIR}/${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
        CONFIG_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/${CONFIG_FILE}"
    fi

    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/config/parse_cfg.sh" "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
        >&2 echo "Error: Failed to parse config file '${CONFIG_FILE}'."
        exit 1
    fi

    # map config values to build variables
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}"
    if [[ -n "${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_COMPILER}" ]]; then
        CC_FROM_CFG="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_COMPILER}"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_EXTRA_CFLAGS}" ]]; then
        CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_EXTRA_CFLAGS}"
    fi

    # determine CPU name from config file name (e.g., nx27v.cfg -> nx27v)
    CPU=$(basename "${CONFIG_FILE}" .cfg | tr A-Z a-z)

    # shift arguments: $3 becomes extra_flags, $4 becomes output_path
    EXTRA_FLAGS_ARG="${3}"
    OUTPUT_PATH_ARG="${4}"

    # apply the same CPU-specific flags as the normal path
    # (fall through to the CPU case logic below)
else
    # legacy positional argument mode
    EXTRA_FLAGS_ARG=""
    OUTPUT_PATH_ARG=""
fi

#convert CPU name to lower case
if [[ "${1}" != "--config" ]]; then
    CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#Input:
# $1: CPU name (case insensitive)
#       this could be N10/D10/D15/N225/D23/N25/NX25/D25/AX25/A27/AX27/N45/D45/NX45/AX45/NX27V
# $2: Extra compilation flags for the library
#       if this parameter is set, these extra flags will
#       be appended to the default compilation flags
#       ; otherwise, the default compilation flags
#       defined in this script will be applied.
# $3: Specified compiler
#       If this parameter is left to be empty, the
#       nds32le-elf-gcc (for V3),
#       riscv32-elf-gcc (for RV32),
#       riscv64-elf-gcc (for RV64) will be used as default compiler.
# $4: Library output path
#       As thsi parameter is set, the built library and objects will be saved
#       into the user specified path; otherwise, the built library and
#       objects will be saved in the root of this repository.
#
# Example:
#   ./build.sh D10
# Or
#   ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang
###############################################################################

#----- build switches -----
BUILD_LIBNN=1
BUILD_DOC=0
MAKE_INSTALL=1

# common compilation flags
CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#convert CPU name to lower case
CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)

#set the default compilation flags
if [ "${CPU}" == "n10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=n10 -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d10 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d15" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d15 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=4 -fno-auto-inc-dec -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n225" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d23" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-23-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=512 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mpv" ]; then
    if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
        CC_TMP="${3}"
    else
        CC_TMP="riscv32-elf-gcc"
    fi

    ${CC_TMP} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax65" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax66" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "x86" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "x64" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=""
else
    >&2 echo "Usage: build.sh CPU_NAME EXTRA_COMPILATION_FLAGS"
    >&2 echo
    >&2 echo "${CPU} is not a supported CPU. Please input the supported CPU name as below."
    >&2 echo "The supported CPUs are: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV or AX46MPV (case insensitive)"
    exit 1
fi

# compiler
if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
    CC="${3}"
else
    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
fi

# customized optimization for 45-series
if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
fi

# extra compilation flags
if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
fi

# output path
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
else
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
fi

#==================== extra optional flags ====================
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
# -----
# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series
        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"

# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
cd ${LIB_ROOT}
nds_autogen_info.sh "libnn"
if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
fi

if [ ${BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    # take 1/16 processors to build the library parallelly
    nprocessors=$(getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN)
    nprocessors=$((nprocessors / 16))
    if [[ ${nprocessors} -lt 1 ]]; then
        # for the case of total processor number is less than 16
        nprocessors=1;
    fi

    if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
        cd ${LIB_ROOT}

        AR="${CC%-*}-ar"
        AR_FLAG="crD"
        libhvm_branch="ast-v5_4_0-release"
        libnhvm_repo_dir="${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project"
        libhvm_name="libhvm"
        libhvm_build_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm.log"
        libhvm_build_error_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm_error.log"

        # clone the libhvm repo and switch to the proper branch
        echo "**********************************************"
        echo "Clone and update libhvm ..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive --remote
        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        git reset --hard ${libhvm_branch}

        # disable the HVM bank awared
        sed -i 's/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t1/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t0/' ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm/src/common/nds_hvm_config.h

        # build the libhvm
        echo "Start building libhvm ..."
        cp ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/Makefile_libhvm ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        make clean -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*}
        make -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*} EXTRA_CFLAGS="-mno-16-bit" V=1 > ${libhvm_build_log} 2> ${libhvm_build_error_log}
        mv ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm.a ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}
        echo "Building libhvm done ..."
        echo "**********************************************"

        cd ${LIB_ROOT}  # go back to the repo root
    fi

    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Building Andes NN Library ..."
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
    make clean -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
    make -j${nprocessors} -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak CFLAGS="${GLOBAL_CFLAG}" CROSS_COMPILE="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}" CC="${CC}" BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
    ret=$?
    echo
    echo "**********************************************"
    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "Andes NN Library build successful."
    else
        echo "Andes NN Library build failed!!!"
    fi
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
fi

#Document generation
if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CUR_DIR=`pwd`
    cd ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs
    rm -rf ./docs
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Generating document for libnn library ..."
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
    doxygen Doxyfile > log_doxygen.txt && cd docs/latex && make > log_make_latex.txt 2>&1
    mv log_make_latex.txt ${LIB_ROOT}
    cd ..
    mkdir -p pdf
    cp latex/refman.pdf pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf
    cp rtf/refman.rtf rtf/libnn_user_manual.rtf
    cd ${CUR_DIR}
    echo
    echo "Done!"
    echo "Andes NN Library (libnn) User Manual is generated with two format:"
    echo "html folder: Docs/docs/html"
    echo "PDF file: Docs/docs/pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf"
    echo "**********************************************"
fi  #BUILD_DOC

#Installation
if [ ${MAKE_INSTALL} -eq 1 ] && [ ${ret} -eq 0 ]; then
    mkdir -p ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    rm -rf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}/*
    cp -f ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/libnn.a ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Include ${RELEASE_FOLDER}

    if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
        cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs/docs/pdf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    fi
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Andes NN Library installation done!"
    echo " install folder: ${RELEASE_FOLDER}"
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
fi  #MAKE_INSTALL

exit $ret

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#Input:
# $1: CPU name (case insensitive)
#       this could be N10/D10/D15/N225/D23/N25/NX25/D25/AX25/A27/AX27/N45/D45/NX45/AX45/NX27V
# $2: Extra compilation flags for the library
#       if this parameter is set, these extra flags will
#       be appended to the default compilation flags
#       ; otherwise, the default compilation flags
#       defined in this script will be applied.
# $3: Specified compiler
#       If this parameter is left to be empty, the
#       nds32le-elf-gcc (for V3),
#       riscv32-elf-gcc (for RV32),
#       riscv64-elf-gcc (for RV64) will be used as default compiler.
# $4: Library output path
#       As thsi parameter is set, the built library and objects will be saved
#       into the user specified path; otherwise, the built library and
#       objects will be saved in the root of this repository.
#
# Example:
#   ./build.sh D10
# Or
#   ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang
###############################################################################

#----- build switches -----
BUILD_LIBNN=1
BUILD_DOC=0
MAKE_INSTALL=1

# common compilation flags
CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#----- config file support -----
# Usage: ./build.sh --config <config_file> [extra_flags] [output_path]
# When --config is used, $1=--config, $2=config_file, $3=extra_flags, $4=output_path
if [[ "${1}" == "--config" ]]; then
    CONFIG_FILE="${2}"
    if [[ -z "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
        >&2 echo "Error: --config requires a config file path."
        exit 1
    fi

    # resolve config file path relative to this script's directory
    SCRIPT_DIR=$(readlink -f "$(dirname "${0}")")
    if [[ ! -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]] && [[ -f "${SCRIPT_DIR}/${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
        CONFIG_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/${CONFIG_FILE}"
    fi

    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/config/parse_cfg.sh" "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
        >&2 echo "Error: Failed to parse config file '${CONFIG_FILE}'."
        exit 1
    fi

    # map config values to build variables
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}"
    if [[ -n "${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_COMPILER}" ]]; then
        CC_FROM_CFG="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_COMPILER}"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_EXTRA_CFLAGS}" ]]; then
        CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_EXTRA_CFLAGS}"
    fi

    # determine CPU name from config file name (e.g., nx27v.cfg -> nx27v)
    CPU=$(basename "${CONFIG_FILE}" .cfg | tr A-Z a-z)

    # shift arguments: $3 becomes extra_flags, $4 becomes output_path
    EXTRA_FLAGS_ARG="${3}"
    OUTPUT_PATH_ARG="${4}"

    # apply the same CPU-specific flags as the normal path
    # (fall through to the CPU case logic below)
else
    # legacy positional argument mode
    EXTRA_FLAGS_ARG=""
    OUTPUT_PATH_ARG=""
fi

#convert CPU name to lower case
if [[ "${1}" != "--config" ]]; then
    CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)
fi

#set the default compilation flags
if [ "${CPU}" == "n10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=n10 -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d10 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d15" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d15 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=4 -fno-auto-inc-dec -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n225" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d23" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-23-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=512 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mpv" ]; then
    if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
        CC_TMP="${3}"
    else
        CC_TMP="riscv32-elf-gcc"
    fi

    ${CC_TMP} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax65" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax66" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "x86" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "x64" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=""
else
    >&2 echo "Usage: build.sh CPU_NAME EXTRA_COMPILATION_FLAGS"
    >&2 echo
    >&2 echo "${CPU} is not a supported CPU. Please input the supported CPU name as below."
    >&2 echo "The supported CPUs are: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV or AX46MPV (case insensitive)"
    exit 1
fi

# compiler
if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
    CC="${3}"
else
    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
fi

# customized optimization for 45-series
if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
fi

# extra compilation flags
if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
fi

# output path
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
else
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
fi

#==================== extra optional flags ====================
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
# -----
# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series
        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"

# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
cd ${LIB_ROOT}
nds_autogen_info.sh "libnn"
if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
fi

if [ ${BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    # take 1/16 processors to build the library parallelly
    nprocessors=$(getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN)
    nprocessors=$((nprocessors / 16))
    if [[ ${nprocessors} -lt 1 ]]; then
        # for the case of total processor number is less than 16
        nprocessors=1;
    fi

    if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
        cd ${LIB_ROOT}

        AR="${CC%-*}-ar"
        AR_FLAG="crD"
        libhvm_branch="ast-v5_4_0-release"
        libnhvm_repo_dir="${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project"
        libhvm_name="libhvm"
        libhvm_build_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm.log"
        libhvm_build_error_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm_error.log"

        # clone the libhvm repo and switch to the proper branch
        echo "**********************************************"
        echo "Clone and update libhvm ..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive --remote
        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        git reset --hard ${libhvm_branch}

        # disable the HVM bank awared
        sed -i 's/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t1/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t0/' ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm/src/common/nds_hvm_config.h

        # build the libhvm
        echo "Start building libhvm ..."
        cp ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/Makefile_libhvm ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        make clean -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*}
        make -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*} EXTRA_CFLAGS="-mno-16-bit" V=1 > ${libhvm_build_log} 2> ${libhvm_build_error_log}
        mv ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm.a ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}
        echo "Building libhvm done ..."
        echo "**********************************************"

        cd ${LIB_ROOT}  # go back to the repo root
    fi

    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Building Andes NN Library ..."
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
    make clean -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
    make -j${nprocessors} -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak CFLAGS="${GLOBAL_CFLAG}" CROSS_COMPILE="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}" CC="${CC}" BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
    ret=$?
    echo
    echo "**********************************************"
    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "Andes NN Library build successful."
    else
        echo "Andes NN Library build failed!!!"
    fi
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
fi

#Document generation
if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CUR_DIR=`pwd`
    cd ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs
    rm -rf ./docs
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Generating document for libnn library ..."
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
    doxygen Doxyfile > log_doxygen.txt && cd docs/latex && make > log_make_latex.txt 2>&1
    mv log_make_latex.txt ${LIB_ROOT}
    cd ..
    mkdir -p pdf
    cp latex/refman.pdf pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf
    cp rtf/refman.rtf rtf/libnn_user_manual.rtf
    cd ${CUR_DIR}
    echo
    echo "Done!"
    echo "Andes NN Library (libnn) User Manual is generated with two format:"
    echo "html folder: Docs/docs/html"
    echo "PDF file: Docs/docs/pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf"
    echo "**********************************************"
fi  #BUILD_DOC

#Installation
if [ ${MAKE_INSTALL} -eq 1 ] && [ ${ret} -eq 0 ]; then
    mkdir -p ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    rm -rf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}/*
    cp -f ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/libnn.a ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Include ${RELEASE_FOLDER}

    if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
        cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs/docs/pdf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    fi
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Andes NN Library installation done!"
    echo " install folder: ${RELEASE_FOLDER}"
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
fi  #MAKE_INSTALL

exit $ret

TMP_LIB_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/lib_dir"                  # temporary folder to keep the empty library
EMPTY_LIB="${TMP_LIB_DIR}/${LIB_NAME}_empty.a"
SIZE_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/size.csv"     # keep all functions' code size

#Save input arguments into meaningful variables
CC="${2}"                   #compiler name
SIM_WRAPPER="${3}"          #simulator name
TEST_TARGET="${4}"          #GDB / SID / BOARD
TEST_TYPE="${5}"            #BS3 / DAILY
EXTRA_FLAG="${6}"           #extra flags for selecting multi-lib
LD="${7}"                   #specified linker

# setup the binutil full name
SIZE="${CC%-*}-size"
GCOV="${CC%-*}-gcov"
OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"

#Compilation flags and related info
DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="-O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

input.new_string:
#----- config file support -----
# Usage: ../test.sh --config <config_file> <BS3|DAILY> [extra_flags] [linker]
# When --config is used, $1=--config, $2=config_file, $3=BS3/DAILY, $4=extra_flags, $5=linker
if [[ "${1}" == "--config" ]]; then
    __CFG_FILE="${2}"
    if [[ -z "${__CFG_FILE}" ]]; then
        >&2 echo "Error: --config requires a config file path."
        exit 1
    fi

    # resolve config file path relative to libnn root
    if [[ ! -f "${__CFG_FILE}" ]] && [[ -f "${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/${__CFG_FILE}" ]]; then
        __CFG_FILE="${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/${__CFG_FILE}"
    fi

    source "${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/config/parse_cfg.sh" "${__CFG_FILE}"
    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
        >&2 echo "Error: Failed to parse config file '${__CFG_FILE}'."
        exit 1
    fi

    # map config values to test variables
    CC="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_COMPILER}"
    SIM_WRAPPER="${3}"          # not set from config; user may override or use wrapper
    TEST_TARGET=""
    TEST_TYPE="${3}"            #BS3 / DAILY
    EXTRA_FLAG="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_EXTRA_CFLAGS}"
    LD="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_LINKER}"

    # append any additional extra flags from the command line
    if [[ -n "${4}" ]]; then
        EXTRA_FLAG="${EXTRA_FLAG:+${EXTRA_FLAG} }${4}"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${5}" ]]; then
        LD="${5}"
    fi

    # auto-detect simulator/wrapper from config
    if [[ -n "${CFG_QEMU_BINARY}" ]]; then
        export QEMU_SYS_RV64="${CFG_QEMU_BINARY}"
        export QEMU_SYS_RV32="${CFG_QEMU_BINARY}"
        TEST_TARGET="QEMU"

        # set VEC_CFG for QEMU wrapper config selection
        if [[ -n "${CFG_QEMU_VEC_CFG}" ]]; then
            export VEC_CFG="${CFG_QEMU_VEC_CFG}"
        fi

        # determine the SIM_WRAPPER: look for a QEMU wrapper in the project dir
        __CFG_CPU=$(basename "${__CFG_FILE}" .cfg | tr A-Z a-z)
        __WRAPPER_DIR="${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/${__CFG_CPU}"
        if [[ -d "${__WRAPPER_DIR}" ]]; then
            # try to find a qemu wrapper
            __QEMU_WRAPPER=$(ls "${__WRAPPER_DIR}"/riscv-qemu-wrapper-* 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [[ -n "${__QEMU_WRAPPER}" ]]; then
                SIM_WRAPPER="${__QEMU_WRAPPER}"
            fi
        fi
    elif [[ -n "${CFG_BOARD_ICE_HOST}" ]]; then
        TEST_TARGET="BOARD"
        if [[ -n "${CFG_BOARD_ICE_HOST}" ]]; then
            export MYPC="${CFG_BOARD_ICE_HOST}"
        fi
        if [[ -n "${CFG_BOARD_ICE_PORT}" ]]; then
            export MYPORT="${CFG_BOARD_ICE_PORT}"
        fi
    else
        # host build or SID — use default sim wrapper
        TEST_TARGET="GDB"
        SIM_WRAPPER="${3}"
    fi
else
    # legacy positional argument mode
    true
fi

#Save input arguments into meaningful variables
if [[ "${1}" != "--config" ]]; then
    CC="${2}"                   #compiler name
    SIM_WRAPPER="${3}"          #simulator name
    TEST_TARGET="${4}"          #GDB / SID / BOARD
    TEST_TYPE="${5}"            #BS3 / DAILY
    EXTRA_FLAG="${6}"           #extra flags for selecting multi-lib
    LD="${7}"                   #specified linker
fi
