toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#
# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
# performance.
#
# Input:
#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
#       of libdsp.
#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
#       environment variable before using this script.
#   $3: Simulator name
#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
#   $4: Simulation target
#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
#              will be verified in this mode.
#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
#       used to build the library.
#   $7: Specified linker
#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
#
# Example:
#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
#
#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
#
#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
#
#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
#
#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
#
#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
#
# Note:
#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
#   be used in the accuray test later.
#
################################################################################

#######################################################
# check the coverage of executed functions
#######################################################
function check_coverage {
    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    cd ${GCOV_DIR}

    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
        IFS=-
        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
        IFS=
        coverage_status=""
        is_coverage_fail=0

        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
                    # coverage_status+=""
                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
                        is_coverage_fail=1
                    fi
                fi

                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")
                break
            fi
        done

        for called_fun in ${called_fun_list[@]}; do
            fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
            # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
            if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
                fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
                coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
                    is_coverage_fail=1
                fi
            fi
        done

        if [ ${is_coverage_fail} -eq 1 ]; then
            coverage_fail_list+=("${coverage_status}")
            echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COVERAGE_FAIL_LIST}
            cp $(basename ${gcda/gcda/c.gcov}) ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
        fi

        # write coverage results into file
        echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COV_CSV}
    done

    # go back to previous path
    cd ${CURRENT_PATH}
}

#######################################################
# collect the code size of each section
#   $1: test function name
#######################################################
calculate_code_size () {
    local log_file build_cmd tmp_size_log

    echo -n "${1}," >> ${SIZE_CSV}
    log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}_empty.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG} -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1_empty.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${EMPTY_LIB} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    # build the executable
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}

    # get the size info
    tmp_size_log="${LOG_DIR}/size.log"
    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx       | tail -1 > ${tmp_size_log}
    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}_empty.adx | tail -1 >> ${tmp_size_log}

    # calculate the size of each section
    awk '
        NR==1 {split($0,non_empty)}
        NR==2 {split($0,empty)}
        END {for(i=1;i<=5;i++) printf "%u,", non_empty[i] - empty[i]}
    ' ${tmp_size_log} >> ${SIZE_CSV}
    echo >> ${SIZE_CSV}  #new line
}

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VLN8 not enabled
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${1} =~ s4 ]]; then
        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
        return
    fi

    log "--- $1 ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        # dump the disassembly
        ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/t_${1}.objdump"

        log "Start at $(date)"
        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
        ret=$?
        log "End at $(date)"

        # check correctness
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
            # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
            # code even if it enounters some issues.)
            # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
            # the test program is executed.
            grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log_file} &> /dev/null
            if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
                accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
                echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log_file}
            else
                accuracy_pass_num=$((accuracy_pass_num + 1))
            fi

            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
                #dump inst/cycle into csv
                echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${PERF_CSV}
                grep -w 'inst' ${log_file}  | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
                grep -w 'cycle' ${log_file} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
                echo >> ${PERF_CSV}  #new line
            fi

            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
                # following parser will work properly only as below assumptions
                # are valid
                #   (1) each parameter is seperated with semicolon except the
                #       last one
                #   (2) the parameters are surrounded by the "param_hint_begin"
                #       and "param_hint_end"

                #dump the parameters into csv
                local param_hint_begin="___PARAMETERS_BEGIN___"
                local param_hint_end="___PARAMETERS_END___"
                local tmp_param_str=$(sed -n "/${param_hint_begin}/,/${param_hint_end}/p" ${log_file})

                # remove all newlines
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\r'/}
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\n'/}
                # remove hint string
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_begin}/}
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_end}/,}
                # make each parameter being in one line
                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//;/;$'\n'}
                echo -e "$2,$1,${tmp_param_str}" >> ${PARAM_CSV}
            fi
        else
            accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
        fi
    else
        # build the executable fail
        accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi

    if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
        calculate_code_size "${1}"
    fi
}

###############################################################################
# build and run the target configuration diagnosis program
#  $1: source file name of the program
###############################################################################
function target_cfg_diagnosis {
    local src_fname="target_cfg_diagnosis"
    log "--- ${src_fname} ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${src_fname}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -o ${ADX_DIR}/$src_fname.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/$src_fname.c ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}

    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        log "Start at $(date)"
        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${src_fname}.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
        ret=$?
        log "End at $(date)"
    fi
}

#######################################################
# build the executables
#   $1: test function name
#######################################################
function build_executable {
    log "--- $1 ---"
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"

    #use the linker specified by the user
    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
        build_cmd+=" -fuse-ld=${LD}"
    fi

    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
    if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} != 0 ]]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
        # build the executable fail
        accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

#######################################################
# Some function's algorithm implementaions are hardware
# configuration dependant; thus, there are mutiple
# algoirhtms for different hardware configurations and
# the code coverage is impossible to be 100% as executing
# the function under one hardware configuration. Here we
# execute these functions with all hardware configuration
# simulator warpper to make the coverage reach 100%.
#######################################################
function run_special_case_for_rvv () {
    local sim_wrapper_list=($(ls ${SIM_WRAPPER%v*}*))
    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/special_case.log"

    for sim_wrapper in "${sim_wrapper_list[@]}"; do
      for fun in "${SPECIAL_CASE_RVV[@]}"; do
          echo "${sim_wrapper} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${fun}.adx" >> ${log_file}
          ${sim_wrapper} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${fun}.adx >> ${log_file}
      done
    done
}

#######################################################
# Build the library
#   $1: flags used to build the library
#######################################################
build_lib () {
    local build_lib_stdout build_lib_stderr
    build_lib_stdout=${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/build_lib.log
    build_lib_stderr=${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/build_lib_error.log

    log "****************************************************"
    log "Build libnn ..."
    start_time
    log "    LIB_CFLAGS= ${1}"
    log "    Output=${build_lib_stdout}"
    log "    Error=${build_lib_stderr}"
    log

    ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${1}" "${CC}" "${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}" > "${build_lib_stdout}" 2> "${build_lib_stderr}"
    if [[ ${CC} =~ "linux" ]] && [[ ! ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "-static" ]]; then
        # build the dynamic library for glibc toolcahin ...
        local all_obj_list=$(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.o)
        local build_shared_lib_cmd="${CC} -shared -o ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/${LIB_NAME}.so ${all_obj_list}"
        echo ${build_shared_lib_cmd} >> "${build_lib_stdout}"
        ${build_shared_lib_cmd} >> "${build_lib_stdout}" 2> "${build_lib_stderr}"
    fi
    build_lib_result=$?

    if [ ${build_lib_result} == 0 ]; then
        log "Build library done."
    else
        log "Build library FAIL !!!"
    fi
    end_time
    log "****************************************************"
}

function log () {
    echo "$*" >> ${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/${TEST_LOG} 2>&1
}

function start_time () {
    log "Start at `date`"
    S_TIME=`date +%s`
}

function end_time () {
    E_TIME=`date +%s`
    log "End at `date`, takes $((E_TIME-S_TIME)) seconds."
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
#----- EDITABLE switches begin -----
ENA_TEST_ALL=1
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
ENA_CONV_TEST=0
ENA_FC_TEST=0
ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
ENA_UTIL_TEST=0

ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # collect instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV=0     # collect parameters into csv
ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE=0     # measure the code size of each function (as this is enabled, the
                            # coverage checking will be disabled)
#----- EDITABLE switches end -----

#----- NON-EDITABLE global variables begin -----
# lib name
LIB_NAME="libnn"

#Path info
CURRENT_PATH=$(pwd)                                 #position of user invokes this script
LIB_ROOT_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)         #position of libnn repository root folder
WRAPPTER_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CURRENT_PATH}/..)     #the wrapper position of BS3
UNIT_TEST_PATH="${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/Examples/unit_func"
#export each cpu project here to make sure the sim wrapper could be found in daily test ???
# export PATH=$( find ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/ -type d -printf ":%p" ):$PATH

#Output files/folders
TIME_STR=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
TEST_LOG="test_sh_${TIME_STR}.log"      #save the ouput message of this script.
TEST_LOG_DIR="test_sh_${TIME_STR}"
PERF_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/performance.csv"
PARAM_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/parameter.csv"
LOG_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/log"
ADX_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/adx"           # collect the executables into TEST_LOG_DIR
OBJDUMP_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/objdump"   # collect the disassembly into TEST_LOG_DIR
GCOV_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/gcov"         # temporary folder to keep gcov outputs
FAILED_GCOV_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/faild_gcov"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"
ACCURACY_FAIL_LIST="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/accuracy_fail_list"
COVERAGE_FAIL_LIST="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/coverage_fail_list"
COV_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/coverage.csv"  # keep all functions' code coverage
# COV_FAIL_LIST="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/coverage_fail_list"
LIB_OBJ_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/lib_objs"                 # all functions' object file
TMP_LIB_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/lib_dir"                  # temporary folder to keep the empty library
EMPTY_LIB="${TMP_LIB_DIR}/${LIB_NAME}_empty.a"
SIZE_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/size.csv"     # keep all functions' code size

#Save input arguments into meaningful variables
CC="${2}"                   #compiler name
SIM_WRAPPER="${3}"          #simulator name
TEST_TARGET="${4}"          #GDB / SID / BOARD
TEST_TYPE="${5}"            #BS3 / DAILY
EXTRA_FLAG="${6}"           #extra flags for selecting multi-lib
LD="${7}"                   #specified linker

# setup the binutil full name
SIZE="${CC%-*}-size"
GCOV="${CC%-*}-gcov"
OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"

#Compilation flags and related info
DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="-O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#default CPU option
if [[ "${CC}" == "nds32"* ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="d10"
    else
        CPU_NAME="n10"
    fi
elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv32"* ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="a45"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="a25"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="n45"
    else
        CPU_NAME="n25"
    fi
elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv64"* ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="ax45"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="ax25"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=100"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=100"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="nx27v"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="nx45"
    else
        CPU_NAME="nx25"
    fi
fi

# add the mtune option for 25-series
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na](x?)25$ ]] || [[ ${CPU_NAME} == "d25" ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -mtune=andes-25-series"
fi

# enable ENA_MEASURE_PERF, ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV and ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV when called from test_perf.sh
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} == *"-DENA_TEST_PERF"* ]]; then
    ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1
    ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=1
    ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV=1
fi

#test sat-ext when using V3 toolcahin and without enabling -mext-dsp
#(sat-ext will be tested after plain C)
# if [[ ${CC} == "nds32"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} != *"-mext-dsp"* ]];
#     SAT_LIB_CFLAGS="DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS -DENA_EXT_SAT_INST"
#     TEST_SAT_EXT="1"
# else
#     TEST_SAT_EXT="0"
# fi

#check code coverage only on daily test
#note. the code coverage testing is not supported on board
if [ "${TEST_TYPE}" == "DAILY" ] && [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(SID|QEMU)$ ]] && [[ "${CC}" != *"clang" ]] && [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 0 ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} --coverage -DGCOV"
    CODE_COVERAGE="1"
else
    CODE_COVERAGE="0"
fi

DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -Wl,-gc-sections"
if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    # only enable the performance counter for elf toolcahins
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS+=" -DPF_COUNTER"

    # enable virtual hosting / semihosting
    if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(BOARD|QEMU|ALIOTH)$ ]]; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mvh"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} == "BOARD" ]] && [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_QILAI_LD"* ]]; then
        # applying the customized linker script for Qilai platform
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx27v_qilai/qilai.ld"
    elif [[ "${CC}" == "nds32"* ]]; then
        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V3)
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/d10/nds32.ld"
    elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} != *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "ENA_LM" ]] ; then
        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V5)
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/d25/ae350-xip.ld"
    fi
fi

# app and lib cflags for v-ext
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FS_VS"
    fi

    # As using v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
        # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
        # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    fi
fi

# extra cflags for the environment with HVM
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_HVM"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
fi

# setup the extra flags for the glibc toolchain
if [[ ${CC} =~ "linux" ]] && [[ ! ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "-static" ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS+=" -fpic"
fi

# customized crt0 for v-ext 0.8
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -nostartfiles ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx25v/crt0.s"
fi

#header files and their search path
if [[ ${TEST_TYPE} == BS3 ]]; then
    # As verifying the unit tests with BS3, we should use the header files is
    # the used toolchain instead of those in libnn repo.
    TOOLCHAIN_PATH=$(which ${CC})
    if [[ ${CC} == *elf* ]]; then
        # for elf toolchain
        PREFIX=${CC%-*}
        INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN=$(readlink -f "${TOOLCHAIN_PATH%/*}/../${PREFIX}/include")
    else
        # for linux toolchain
        INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN=$(readlink -f "${TOOLCHAIN_PATH%/*}/../sysroot/usr/include")
    fi
    INC_DIR="-I${INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN} -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
else
    # As verifying the unit tests during developing, we should use the header
    # files in libnn repo; otherwise, the prototypes of new functions can't
    # be found by the compiler.
    INC_DIR="-I${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
fi

#Some identification flags
if [[ ${CC} == "nds32be"* ]]; then
    ENDIAN="be"
else
    ENDIAN="le"
fi

if [[ ${CC} == *"linux"* ]]; then
    TOOLCHAIN_IS_GLIBC="1"
else
    TOOLCHAIN_IS_GLIBC="0"
fi

#select the library to be linked (from built-in or manual building library)
if [ "${TEST_TYPE}" == "BS3" ];then
    # now the linked libray name needs to be passed manually as linking to the
    # built-in libraries (owing to there is not enough information to
    # distinguish the ELEN32 and ELEN64 version v-ext library)
    LINKED_LIB=""
else
    LINKED_LIB="-lnn -L${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}"
fi

# setup the linking library and header files searching path for HVM
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then

mkdir -p ${ADX_DIR}
mkdir -p ${OBJDUMP_DIR}
mkdir -p ${GCOV_DIR}
mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR}
mkdir -p ${TMP_LIB_DIR}

# clean old temp files/executables

rm -f ${OBJDUMP_DIR}/*.adx

# below functions wil be skipped as measuring the performance
PF_SKIP_CASE=(
    "get_version_libnn:util"
)

if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${ACTIVATION[@]/%/:activation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_BASIC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${BASIC[@]/%/:basic}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONCATE_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONCATE[@]/%/:concatenation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONV_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONV[@]/%/:convolution}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_FC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${FC[@]/%/:fully_connected}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_POOLING_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${POOLING[@]/%/:pooling}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${SOFTMAX[@]/%/:softmax}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_UTIL_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${UTIL[@]/%/:util}")
fi

#remove non-unit-stride test cases
# if [ ${ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY} == 1 ]; then
#     for FUN_NAME in "${NON_UNIT_STRIDE[@]}";
#     do
#         test_list=(${test_list[@]/$FUN_NAME})
#     done
# fi

# remove the test cases as measuring the performance
if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -eq 1 ]]; then
    for FUN_NAME in "${PF_SKIP_CASE[@]}";
    do
        test_list=(${test_list[@]/$FUN_NAME})
    done
fi

# record whether the zfh extension is enabled
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} == *"-mzfh"* ]]; then
    ENA_FP16=1
else
    ENA_FP16=0
fi

# record whether the VL4 extension is enabled
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_VL4 ]]; then
    ENA_VL4=1
else
    ENA_VL4=0
fi

##################################################
# Build the library with empty functions for measuring the code size. Remember
# to build this library before building the non-empty version library, or it
# will influence the code coveratge checking results.
##################################################
if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    build_lib "${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -DENA_EMPTY_FUN"

    # move the empty version library to the temporary folder to avoid being
    # removed later as bulding the non-empty version library
    mv "${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/libnn.a" "${EMPTY_LIB}"
fi

##################################################
# Build the non-empty library.
##################################################
if [[ ${TEST_TYPE} == DAILY ]]; then
    build_lib "${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
fi

##################################################
# start to execute the unit tests
##################################################
if [ ${build_lib_result} == 0 ]; then
    log
    log "****************************************************"
    log "Begin the accuracy and performance testing ..."
    log

    #---- enable the configuration diagnosis program for ELF toolcahins -----
    if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] && [[ ! ${CC} == *linux* ]]; then
        target_cfg_diagnosis
    fi

    #TODO: the inst/cycle field needs to be mofiled (they may not show just one time)
    if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
        #init csv title
        if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} == 1 ]; then
            echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${PERF_CSV}
        fi
    fi

    if [ ${ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
        echo "group, function, parameters" > ${PARAM_CSV}
    fi

    if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
        # init the csv title
        echo "fun_name,text,code,rodata,data,bss" > ${SIZE_CSV}
    fi

    #----- enable the LM switch -----
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
        sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
    fi

    for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
        fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
        fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)

        if [[ ${CC} == *linux* ]] && [[ ${TEST_TARGET} == BOARD ]]; then
            # As using the linux toolchain and the test target is board, this
            # script is only used to generate the executables.
            build_executable "${fun_name}"

            # copy the required binary files for the unit tests
            cp -r ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/bin ${TEST_LOG_DIR}

            # copy the running executables for glibc on board script
            cp ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/run_executable_for_glibc.sh ${TEST_LOG_DIR}

            # enable the write permission for others
            chmod o+w ${TEST_LOG_DIR}
        else
            run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
        fi
    done

    #----- restore the LM switch -----
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
        sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
    fi

    if [ ${CODE_COVERAGE} == "1" ]; then
        if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
            run_special_case_for_rvv
        fi

        mkdir -p ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
        check_coverage

        if [ ${#coverage_fail_list[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
            rm -rf ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
        fi
    fi

    #clean gcov output files
    rm -rf ${GCOV_DIR}
    rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.gc{da,no}
    rm -f ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gc{da,no};

    log
    log "End of accuracy and performance test."
    log "****************************************************"
fi

##################################################
# show accuracy and coverage results only when building library successfully
##################################################
if [ ${build_lib_result} == 0 ]; then
    #show result summary
    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
    executed_num=$((total_num - accuracy_unsupport_num))
    log
    log "========== summary =========="
    log "Total functions:       ${#test_list[@]}"
    log "Executed functions:    ${executed_num}"
    if [[ ${executed_num} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log "Passed functions:      ${accuracy_pass_num} (pass rate: 0.00%)"
    else
        log "Passed functions:      ${accuracy_pass_num} (pass rate: $(echo ${accuracy_pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l | xargs printf "%.2f")%)"
    fi
    log "Unsupported functions: ${accuracy_unsupport_num}"
    log

    # show accuracy fail list
    if [ ${#accuracy_fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
        log
        log "--- failed funcion list: ---"
        for FUN_NAME in "${accuracy_fail_list[@]}"; do
            log ${FUN_NAME}
            echo ${FUN_NAME} >> ${ACCURACY_FAIL_LIST}
        done
        log
    fi

    # show coverage fail list
    if [ ${#coverage_fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
        log
        log "--- coverage failed function list: ---"
        for FUN_AND_COV in "${coverage_fail_list[@]}"; do
            log ${FUN_AND_COV}
        done
        log
    fi
fi

#generate error log (diff_log)
FINAL_RESULT=$(( ${build_lib_result} + ${#accuracy_fail_list[@]} ))
if [ ${FINAL_RESULT} -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "${FINAL_RESULT}" > ${ERROR_LOG}
    cp -f ${ERROR_LOG} ${TEST_LOG_DIR}/${ERROR_LOG}
    cp -f ${ERROR_LOG} ../${ERROR_LOG}
fi

log "***************************************************************************************"
log "FINAL_RESULT=${FINAL_RESULT}"
log End at `date`
FINISH_TIME=`date +%s`
log Total time is $((FINISH_TIME-BEGIN_TIME)) seconds.
log All log file are saved at ${TEST_LOG_DIR} folder.
log "    Full path=${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}"
log libnn accuracy testing end.
log "***************************************************************************************"

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# config/run.sh — Wrapper that reads an INI-style .cfg file and invokes
#                 build.sh / test.sh with the correct positional arguments.
#
# This script does NOT modify build.sh or test.sh. It translates config values
# into the positional arguments and environment variables those scripts expect.
#
# Usage:
#   config/run.sh build  <config_file> [extra_cflags] [output_path]
#   config/run.sh test   <config_file> <BS3|DAILY> [extra_flags] [linker]
#
# Config file format (INI-style):
#   # comment
#   [section]
#   key = value        # inline comment
#
# Supported sections and keys:
#   [cpu]
#     name             — CPU name passed to build.sh $1 (e.g., nx27v, d25, x86)
#
#   [toolchain]
#     prefix           — toolchain prefix (e.g., riscv64-elf-)
#     compiler         — full compiler name or path (e.g., riscv64-elf-gcc)
#     linker           — linker name (e.g., lld); optional
#     extra_cflags     — extra flags appended to build.sh $2 / test.sh $6
#
#   [sim]
#     wrapper          — simulator wrapper name/path (test.sh $3)
#     target           — GDB, SID, BOARD, or QEMU (test.sh $4)
#
#   [qemu]
#     binary           — QEMU binary path; exported as QEMU_SYS_RV64/RV32
#     vec_cfg          — vector config (e.g., zve64d); exported as VEC_CFG
#
#   [board]
#     ice_host         — ICEman host; exported as MYPC
#     ice_port         — ICEman port; exported as MYPORT
#
# Example config (config/ax45mpv.cfg):
#   [cpu]
#   name = ax45mpv
#
#   [toolchain]
#   compiler = riscv64-elf-gcc
#   extra_cflags = -mext-vector -mtune=andes-45-series
#
#   [sim]
#   wrapper = riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512
#   target = QEMU
#
#   [qemu]
#   binary = /opt/qemu/bin/qemu-system-riscv64
#   vec_cfg = zve64d
#
# Example invocations:
#   config/run.sh build config/ax45mpv.cfg
#   config/run.sh build config/ax45mpv.cfg "-funroll-loops"
#   config/run.sh test  config/ax45mpv.cfg BS3
#   config/run.sh test  config/ax45mpv.cfg DAILY "" lld
################################################################################

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(readlink -f "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")")
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f "${SCRIPT_DIR}/..")

#--- INI parser ---
# Reads the config file and exports CFG_<SECTION>_<KEY> variables.
parse_cfg() {
    local cfg_file="${1}"
    local section=""

    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "${line}" ]]; do
        # trim whitespace
        line="${line#"${line%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
        line="${line%"${line##*[![:space:]]}"}"

        # skip empty / comment lines
        [[ -z "${line}" ]] && continue
        [[ "${line}" == \#* ]] && continue

        # section header
        if [[ "${line}" =~ ^\[([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\]$ ]]; then
            section="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
            continue
        fi

        # key = value
        if [[ "${line}" =~ ^([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*(.*) ]]; then
            local key="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
            local val="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"

            # strip inline comments (# preceded by whitespace)
            val="${val%%[[:space:]]#*}"
            # strip trailing whitespace
            val="${val%"${val##*[![:space:]]}"}"

            local sec_upper key_upper
            sec_upper=$(echo "${section}" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')
            key_upper=$(echo "${key}" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')

            export "CFG_${sec_upper}_${key_upper}=${val}"
        fi
    done < "${cfg_file}"
}

#--- helpers ---
die() { echo "Error: $*" >&2; exit 1; }

usage() {
    echo "Usage:"
    echo "  ${0} build <config_file> [extra_cflags] [output_path]"
    echo "  ${0} test  <config_file> <BS3|DAILY> [extra_flags] [linker]"
    exit 1
}

#--- main ---
[[ $# -lt 2 ]] && usage

MODE="${1}"
CFG_FILE="${2}"
shift 2

# resolve config file path
if [[ ! -f "${CFG_FILE}" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "${LIB_ROOT}/${CFG_FILE}" ]]; then
        CFG_FILE="${LIB_ROOT}/${CFG_FILE}"
    else
        die "Config file '${CFG_FILE}' not found."
    fi
fi

parse_cfg "${CFG_FILE}"

# export QEMU env vars if configured
if [[ -n "${CFG_QEMU_BINARY:-}" ]]; then
    export QEMU_SYS_RV64="${CFG_QEMU_BINARY}"
    export QEMU_SYS_RV32="${CFG_QEMU_BINARY}"
fi
if [[ -n "${CFG_QEMU_VEC_CFG:-}" ]]; then
    export VEC_CFG="${CFG_QEMU_VEC_CFG}"
fi

# export board env vars if configured
if [[ -n "${CFG_BOARD_ICE_HOST:-}" ]]; then
    export MYPC="${CFG_BOARD_ICE_HOST}"
fi
if [[ -n "${CFG_BOARD_ICE_PORT:-}" ]]; then
    export MYPORT="${CFG_BOARD_ICE_PORT}"
fi

# read config values with defaults
CPU_NAME="${CFG_CPU_NAME:-}"
COMPILER="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_COMPILER:-}"
EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_EXTRA_CFLAGS:-}"
LINKER="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_LINKER:-}"
SIM_WRAPPER="${CFG_SIM_WRAPPER:-}"
SIM_TARGET="${CFG_SIM_TARGET:-}"

[[ -z "${CPU_NAME}" ]] && die "[cpu] name is required in config file."

case "${MODE}" in
    build)
        # build.sh $1=CPU  $2=extra_cflags  $3=compiler  $4=output_path
        ARG_EXTRA="${1:-}"
        ARG_OUTPUT="${2:-}"

        # merge extra_cflags: config + command-line
        MERGED_EXTRA="${EXTRA_CFLAGS}"
        if [[ -n "${ARG_EXTRA}" ]]; then
            MERGED_EXTRA="${MERGED_EXTRA:+${MERGED_EXTRA} }${ARG_EXTRA}"
        fi

        echo ">> build.sh ${CPU_NAME} \"${MERGED_EXTRA}\" \"${COMPILER}\" \"${ARG_OUTPUT}\""
        exec "${LIB_ROOT}/build.sh" "${CPU_NAME}" "${MERGED_EXTRA}" "${COMPILER}" "${ARG_OUTPUT}"
        ;;

    test)
        # test.sh $1=""(dummy)  $2=compiler  $3=sim_wrapper  $4=target  $5=BS3/DAILY  $6=extra_flags  $7=linker
        TEST_TYPE="${1:-}"
        ARG_EXTRA="${2:-}"
        ARG_LINKER="${3:-}"

        [[ -z "${TEST_TYPE}" ]] && die "test mode requires BS3 or DAILY as third argument."

        # merge extra_cflags: config + command-line
        MERGED_EXTRA="${EXTRA_CFLAGS}"
        if [[ -n "${ARG_EXTRA}" ]]; then
            MERGED_EXTRA="${MERGED_EXTRA:+${MERGED_EXTRA} }${ARG_EXTRA}"
        fi

        # use command-line linker if provided, else config
        FINAL_LINKER="${ARG_LINKER:-${LINKER}}"

        echo ">> test.sh \"\" \"${COMPILER}\" \"${SIM_WRAPPER}\" \"${SIM_TARGET}\" \"${TEST_TYPE}\" \"${MERGED_EXTRA}\" \"${FINAL_LINKER}\""
        exec "${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh" "" "${COMPILER}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${SIM_TARGET}" "${TEST_TYPE}" "${MERGED_EXTRA}" "${FINAL_LINKER}"
        ;;

    *)
        die "Unknown mode '${MODE}'. Use 'build' or 'test'."
        ;;
esac

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→
     3→#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
     4→RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
     5→                    #  - 0: standalone mode
     6→                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
     7→#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

     9→OLD_PWD=`pwd`

    31→if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    32→    echo ""

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi

The performance CSV only has 14 entries — all f16 functions marked `x,x,` (skipped). The non-f16 functions don't appear in the CSV at all. That's because `ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV` is `0` by default in test.sh, so cycle/instruction counts are only collected when `ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1`.

The performance CSV is not the right place to look for pass/fail. Let me check the actual results:

content.text:
The test is still running — the log cuts off mid-way through convolution tests. But your question about `performance.csv` not showing cycles:

The cycle counts are **not collected by default**. In `test.sh`, `ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0` and `ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0` by default. The performance CSV only gets inst/cycle data when `ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1`, which is only enabled when `EXTRA_FLAG` contains `-DENA_TEST_PERF` (typically invoked via `test_perf.sh`).

The only entries in `performance.csv` are the skipped f16 functions (marked `x,x,`) because those get logged regardless when `ENA_FP16=0`.

If you want cycle counts, you'd need to pass `-DENA_TEST_PERF` in the extra flags, or run via `test_perf.sh`.
